primary_question: How scarce is the physical world?

cache.source_policy: Refreshed from Yahoo Finance public chart endpoints. Homepage build reads this cache and does not fetch commodities live.

doctrine.primary_rule: Render commodities as physical constraint evidence, not a commodity trading dashboard.
doctrine.purpose: Expose energy, industrial, food, and monetary-metal scarcity signals that can shape current and emerging regimes.
doctrine.interpretation_position: Secondary to raw commodity data, lens percentiles, and cross-asset confirmation.

macro_gate_contribution.caveat: Mission 5G covers six core commodity signals. Inventories, futures curves, shipping, producer costs, and regional power data remain missing.

commodities_stack.title: Commodities: energy, industrial, food, monetary metal

monetary_metal.question: Are gold and silver expressing monetary concern (real-rate/debasement pressure) or industrial optimism?

capital_behavior.strategy_posture: Use commodities to identify scarcity pressure and emerging industrial buildout, not as standalone inflation commentary.
capital_behavior.invalidation: Commodity pressure is not durable unless confirmed by inventories, futures curves, demand data, and inflation/FX context.

web_summary.lead: Commodities show whether physical constraints are tightening across energy, industry, food, and monetary metal demand.
web_summary.current_read: Oil 74.25, natural gas 3.217, uranium 41.66, copper 6.13, agri 27.62, silver 58.755, gold 4086.9.

analysis.statement: Commodities matter when physical scarcity, buildout demand, or monetary-metal demand changes capital allocation across inflation, real assets, and industrial equities.
analysis.invalidation: Do not treat commodity price strength as structural unless multiple lenses confirm and inventory/futures data support scarcity.

limitations: Yahoo chart API is public/unofficial. Inventory data, futures curves, shipping data, regional power prices, and producer cost curves remain missing evidence.
